mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so equity valuations re-rate upward and the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign relation reverses in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating labor income and higher recession risk, which compresses risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average 20-day return after the unemployment rise is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth accelerates while unemployment is not rising, the market reads stronger end-demand and better earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either single proxy alone, or if positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods where RSAFS is rising and UNRATE is flat-to-down.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which supports cyclical earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not positive on average when INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average, or if the signal loses sign consistency in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent range signals housing weakness and slower household spending, which lowers equity expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the post-trigger 20-day return distribution is not meaningfully negative, or if more than half of triggered events are followed by positive S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is running above target while FEDFUNDS is still elevated, tighter real policy raises discount rates and threatens margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term CPI inflation with high FEDFUNDS does not predict lower 60-day returns, or if returns remain positive despite both series being elevated for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad demand momentum that improves revenue expectations across sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1-RSAFS strength does not outperform either proxy individually, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in expansion episodes defined by both series rising together.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor-market stress regime, a low unemployment rate stops being supportive and instead precedes earnings pressure and multiple compression, so the S&P 500 underperforms over the next week after a sharp unemployment increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month and PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the combination of rising unemployment and falling payrolls fails to produce a negative average return.
